---
title: "Differential & LSEA"
author: "alexander-gysin (alexander.gysin@usz.ch)"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%d %B, %Y')`"
output: workflowr::wflow_html
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: inline
---

```{r setup_boxes, include=FALSE}
source(here::here("code", "00_config.R"))
```

# Setup and Import

`r what_this_does("Loaded statistical packages including logistf for Firths penalized regression.", "Imported the pre-cleaned clinical spine, clinical data dictionary, and sparse lipidomics matrix.", "Sourced custom lipid omics engines.")`

```{r setup_and_import, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork)
library(here)
library(logistf)    # Firth's logistic regression
library(gtsummary)  # Clinical tables
library(kableExtra) # HTML tables

# Knitr Config
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

# Output paths
current_file <- "04b_lipidomics_differential"
output_dir_data <- here::here("output", current_file)
if (!dir.exists(output_dir_data)) dir.create(output_dir_data, recursive = TRUE)

# Asset paths (Public/GitHub accessible)
assets_dir <- here::here("docs", "assets", current_file)
if (!dir.exists(assets_dir)) dir.create(assets_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Source Configuration & Engines
source(here::here("code", "03_lipidomics_engines.R"))

# Load Pre-Cleaned Data
master_spine <- readRDS(here::here("output", "01b_clinical_feature_engineering", "curated_clinical_spine.rds"))
data_dictionary <- readRDS(here::here("output", "01b_clinical_feature_engineering", "clinical_data_dictionary.rds"))
lipid_mat <- readRDS(here::here("output", "01a_data_import_and_qc", "raw_lipid_matrix.rds"))
mean_lod_df <- readRDS(here::here("output", "01a_data_import_and_qc", "lipid_mean_lod.rds"))

# Dynamic Feature Count
total_features <- nrow(lipid_mat)
```

# Configurations

`r what_this_does("Configured the Sparsity Triage thresholds and parameterized the Differential Expression routing logic, including the continuous binning toggle.")`

```{r configurations}
# 1. Sparsity Triage Configs ---------------------------
sparsity_configs <- list(
  global_triage = list(
    min_detect_rate = 0.10, 
    binarize_data = TRUE    # Convert to 1/0 (Present/Missing)
  )
)

# 2. DEA Routing Configs ---------------------------
dea_configs <- list(
  job_active_vs_nonactive = list(
    title = "Active vs. Non-Active",
    test_var = "cohort_group",
    target_groups = c("Active"),
    ref_groups = c("Non-Active"),
    bin_continuous = FALSE,  
    fdr_cutoff = 0.05,
    fc_cutoff = 1.0
  ),
  
  job_ssc_vs_ctrl = list(
    title = "SSc vs. Control",
    test_var = "cohort_group",
    target_groups = c("Active", "Non-Active"),
    ref_groups = c("Control"),
    bin_continuous = FALSE, 
    fdr_cutoff = 0.05,
    fc_cutoff = 1.0
  ),
  
  job_cluster_1_vs_2 = list(
    title = "Cluster 1 vs Cluster 2",
    test_var = "kmeans_Cluster",
    target_groups = c("Cluster 2"),
    ref_groups = c("Cluster 1"),
    bin_continuous = FALSE,
    fdr_cutoff = 0.05,
    fc_cutoff = 1.0,
    external_data = list(
      file_path = here::here("output", "03c_proteomics_clustering", "Clustering", "job_svc", "job_svc_Master_Assignments.rds"),
      join_col = "Subject_ID"
    )
  ),
  
    job_med_ccb = list(
    title = "Calcium Channel Blockers",
    test_var = "Med_ccb",
    target_groups = c("yes"),
    ref_groups = c("no"),
    bin_continuous = FALSE,
    fdr_cutoff = 0.05,
    fc_cutoff = 1.0
  ),
  
    job_med_asa = list(
    title = "Acetylsalicylic Acid",
    test_var = "Med_asa",
    target_groups = c("yes"),
    ref_groups = c("no"),
    bin_continuous = FALSE,
    fdr_cutoff = 0.05,
    fc_cutoff = 1.0
  )
)

# 3. Plot Configurations ---------------------------
plot_configs <- list(
  volcano = list(
    point_size = 2,
    alpha = 0.8,
    color_sig = "firebrick",
    color_ns = "grey70"
  )
)
```

# Sparsity Triage & Binarization

`r what_this_does("Evaluates global missingness across the matrix. Binarizes the data into Presence/Absence and quarantines ultra-sparse features to save statistical power, reserving them for downstream family-level aggregation.")`

```{r sparsity_triage_and_binarization, results='asis'}
# Triage Math ---------------------------
conf <- sparsity_configs$global_triage

# 1. Binarize Matrix
if(conf$binarize_data) {
  binary_mat <- ifelse(is.na(lipid_mat) | lipid_mat == 0, 0, 1)
} else {
  binary_mat <- lipid_mat
}

# 2. Calculate global detection rates
detection_rates <- rowMeans(binary_mat, na.rm = TRUE)

# 3. Split features
testable_lipids <- names(detection_rates[detection_rates >= conf$min_detect_rate])
ultra_sparse_lipids <- names(detection_rates[detection_rates < conf$min_detect_rate])

# 4. Create final working matrices
mat_testable <- binary_mat[testable_lipids, , drop = FALSE]
mat_testable_raw <- lipid_mat[testable_lipids, , drop = FALSE] 

# 5. Export ultra-sparse list for later Lipid Set Enrichment (LSEA)
saveRDS(ultra_sparse_lipids, file.path(output_dir_data, "ultra_sparse_lipids_for_LSEA.rds"))

cat(c(sprintf("### Triage Summary {.unlisted .unnumbered}\n")), 
    sprintf("* **Total Lipids Input:** %d\n", nrow(lipid_mat)),
    sprintf("* **Testable Lipids (Passed %.0f%% threshold):** %d\n", conf$min_detect_rate * 100, length(testable_lipids)),
    sprintf("* **Ultra-Sparse Lipids (Quarantined for LSEA):** %d\n\n", length(ultra_sparse_lipids)))

# Cleanup ---------------------------
rm(detection_rates, conf)
```

# Differential {.tabset}

`r what_this_does("Dynamically queries the Data Dictionary to determine metadata classes. Routes categorical/binned variables to Fisher's Exact Test and continuous variables to Firth's Penalized Regression. Compiles unified Volcano Plots and saves independent stat modules.")`

```{r Differential, results='asis'}
# Global storage for LSEA ranking
ranked_results_list <- list()

for (job_id in names(dea_configs)) {
  job <- dea_configs[[job_id]]
  
  cat(sprintf("\n\n## %s\n\n", job$title))
  
  # 1. External Data Injection ---------------------------
  temp_spine <- master_spine
  if (!is.null(job$external_data)) {
    ext_df <- readRDS(job$external_data$file_path)
    temp_spine <- temp_spine %>% dplyr::inner_join(ext_df, by = job$external_data$join_col)
    
    if (!(job$test_var %in% data_dictionary$Variable)) {
      data_dictionary <- bind_rows(data_dictionary, data.frame(Variable = job$test_var, Class = "Categorical"))
    }
  }
  
  # 2. Dictionary Lookup & Pre-processing ---------------------------
  var_class <- data_dictionary %>% filter(Variable == job$test_var) %>% pull(Class) %>% tolower()
  var_class <- if (length(var_class) > 0) var_class[1] else "unknown"
  
  temp_spine <- temp_spine %>% filter(!is.na(.data[[job$test_var]]))
  target_engine <- "fisher"
  
  cat(sprintf("### Overview: %s {.unlisted .unnumbered}\n\n", job$title))
  
  if (var_class %in% c("numeric", "integer", "double", "continuous")) {
    if (isTRUE(job$bin_continuous)) {
      median_val <- median(temp_spine[[job$test_var]], na.rm = TRUE)
      temp_spine <- temp_spine %>% mutate(!!job$test_var := ifelse(.data[[job$test_var]] > median_val, "High", "Low"))
      target_engine <- "fisher"
      cat(sprintf("*Note: %s was binned at median (%.2f) and routed to Fisher's Exact Test.*\n\n", job$test_var, median_val))
    } else {
      target_engine <- "firth"
      cat(sprintf("*Note: %s is continuous and was routed to Firth's Penalized Logistic Regression.*\n\n", job$test_var))
    }
  } else {
      cat(sprintf("*Note: %s is categorical/binary and was routed to Fisher's Exact Test.*\n\n", job$test_var))
  }
  
  # 3. Execute Statistical Wrapper ---------------------------
  res <- wrap_sparse_dea(
    mat = mat_testable, 
    clin_df = temp_spine, 
    config = job, 
    engine = target_engine,
    job_name = job_id,
    out_dir = output_dir_data,
    assets_dir = assets_dir,                  
    project_colors_func = get_project_colors 
  )
  
  if (res$status == "success") {
    
    ranked_results_list[[job_id]] <- res$tables$full_results
    saveRDS(res$tables$full_results, file.path(output_dir_data, sprintf("%s_full_stats.rds", job_id)))
    
    # Render Output Table
    cat(sprintf("### Significant Lipids: %s {.unlisted .unnumbered}\n\n", job$title))
    if (nrow(res$tables$significant_features) > 0) {
      tbl <- head(res$tables$significant_features, 15) %>% 
        mutate(Log2OR = round(Log2OR, 2), P_Value = formatC(P_Value, format = "e", digits = 2), FDR = formatC(FDR, format = "e", digits = 2)) %>%
        kableExtra::kbl(format = "html", caption = sprintf("Top Hits: %s", job$title)) %>% 
        kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"))
      print(tbl)
      cat("\n\n") 
    } else {
      cat("*No significant features survived FDR correction.* \n\n")
    }
    
    cat(sprintf("### Volcano Plot: %s {.unlisted .unnumbered}\n\n", job$title))
    if (!is.null(res$plots$volcano)) { 
      if (interactive()) { print(res$plots$volcano); cat("\n\n")
      } else { cat(sprintf("<img src='assets/%s/%s_volcano.png' width='100%%' />\n\n", current_file, job_id)); cat("\n\n") }
    }
    
    cat(sprintf("### Heatmap: %s {.unlisted .unnumbered}\n\n", job$title))
    if (!is.null(res$plots$heatmap)) { 
      if (interactive()) { ComplexHeatmap::draw(res$plots$heatmap); cat("\n\n")
      } else { cat(sprintf("<img src='assets/%s/%s_heatmap.png' width='100%%' />\n\n", current_file, job_id)); cat("\n\n") }
    }
    
    cat(sprintf("### Exclusion Report: %s {.unlisted .unnumbered}\n\n", job$title))
    cat(sprintf("Out of the %d globally testable lipids, the following were excluded from this specific contrast due to zero mathematical variance between groups.\n\n", nrow(mat_testable)))
    if (nrow(res$tables$failed_features) > 0) {
      excl_tbl <- res$tables$failed_features %>% select(Lipid, Method) %>% rename(Failure_Reason = Method) %>%
        kableExtra::kbl(format = "html") %>% kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
        kableExtra::scroll_box(height = "300px")
      print(excl_tbl)
      cat("\n\n")
    } else {
      cat("*All lipids possessed sufficient variance to be tested.* \n\n")
    }
    
  } else {
     cat(sprintf("**FAILED:** %s\n\n", res$error_msg))
  }
  rm(temp_spine, var_class, target_engine, res)
}
```

# Lipid Set Enrichment Analysis (LSEA)

`r what_this_does("Executes a Lipid Set Enrichment Analysis (LSEA) using a 'Binary Burden' approach. Aggregates both testable and ultra-sparse lipids by structural family to rescue statistical power. Tests family-level differences using non-parametric Mann-Whitney U statistics.")`

```{r lsea_execution, fig.width=14, fig.height=10, results='asis'}
# 1. Collapse the Binarized Matrix into a Family Burden Matrix
# We use the full binary_mat from the Triage chunk to include ultra-sparse lipids!
mapped_lipids <- data.frame(Mediator = rownames(binary_mat)) %>%
  dplyr::left_join(mean_lod_df %>% select(Mediator, Family) %>% distinct(), by = "Mediator") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Family = ifelse(is.na(Family) | Family == "", "Unclassified", Family))

# Aggregate columns (patients) by taking the sum of the rows (lipids) per Family
family_burden_mat <- apply(binary_mat, 2, function(col) {
  tapply(col, mapped_lipids$Family, sum, na.rm = TRUE)
})

# 2. Execution Loop (Re-uses dea_configs)
for (job_id in names(dea_configs)) {
  job <- dea_configs[[job_id]]
  
  cat(sprintf("\n\n## %s\n\n", job$title))
  
  # External Data Injection
  temp_spine <- master_spine
  if (!is.null(job$external_data)) {
    ext_df <- readRDS(job$external_data$file_path)
    temp_spine <- temp_spine %>% dplyr::inner_join(ext_df, by = job$external_data$join_col)
  }
  
  # Handle Continuous Binning
  temp_spine <- temp_spine %>% filter(!is.na(.data[[job$test_var]]))
  if (isTRUE(job$bin_continuous)) {
    median_val <- median(temp_spine[[job$test_var]], na.rm = TRUE)
    temp_spine <- temp_spine %>% mutate(!!job$test_var := ifelse(.data[[job$test_var]] > median_val, "High", "Low"))
  }
  
  cat(sprintf("### LSEA Burden: %s {.unlisted .unnumbered}\n\n", job$title))
  
  # Call LSEA Wrapper
  res <- wrap_lsea(
    burden_mat = family_burden_mat, 
    clin_df = temp_spine, 
    config = job, 
    job_name = job_id,
    out_dir = output_dir_data,
    assets_dir = assets_dir,
    project_colors_func = get_project_colors
  )
  
  if (res$status == "success") {
    if (!is.null(res$plot)) { 
      if (interactive()) { 
        print(res$plot)
        cat("\n\n")
      } else { 
        cat(sprintf("<img src='assets/%s/%s_LSEA_burden.png' width='100%%' />\n\n", current_file, job_id))
        cat("\n\n") 
      }
    } else {
      cat("*No valid family data to plot.* \n\n")
    }
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("**FAILED:** %s\n\n", res$error_msg))
  }
}
```

# Raw Feature Abundances {.tabset}
`r what_this_does("Generates standalone, high-resolution patchwork grids displaying the raw, log10-transformed intensity distributions for every testable lipid across the requested clinical contrasts. Sorted alphabetically for standardized reference.")`

```{r raw_abundances, fig.width=12, fig.height=18, results='asis'}
for (job_id in names(dea_configs)) {
  job <- dea_configs[[job_id]]
  
  cat(sprintf("\n\n## %s\n\n", job$title))
  
  # 1. External Data Injection
  temp_spine <- master_spine
  if (!is.null(job$external_data)) {
    ext_df <- readRDS(job$external_data$file_path)
    temp_spine <- temp_spine %>% dplyr::inner_join(ext_df, by = job$external_data$join_col)
  }
  
  # 2. Filter NAs and handle binning
  temp_spine <- temp_spine %>% filter(!is.na(.data[[job$test_var]]))
  if (isTRUE(job$bin_continuous)) {
    median_val <- median(temp_spine[[job$test_var]], na.rm = TRUE)
    temp_spine <- temp_spine %>% mutate(!!job$test_var := ifelse(.data[[job$test_var]] > median_val, "High", "Low"))
  }
  
  cat(sprintf("### Jitter Distributions: %s {.unlisted .unnumbered}\n\n", job$title))
  
  # 3. Execute Plotting Wrapper
  res <- wrap_raw_abundances(
    mat_raw = mat_testable_raw, 
    clin_df = temp_spine, 
    config = job, 
    job_name = job_id,
    out_dir = output_dir_data,
    assets_dir = assets_dir,
    project_colors_func = get_project_colors
  )
  
  if (res$status == "success") {
    if (!is.null(res$plot)) { 
      if (interactive()) { 
        print(res$plot)
        cat("\n\n")
      } else { 
        cat(sprintf("<img src='assets/%s/%s_raw_intensities.png' width='100%%' />\n\n", current_file, job_id))
        cat("\n\n") 
      }
    } else {
      cat("*No valid data to plot.* \n\n")
    }
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("**FAILED:** %s\n\n", res$error_msg))
  }
}
```


# Export

`r what_this_does("Aggregates all continuous and categorical statistical outputs, combining them with the quarantined ultra-sparse features, and exports a unified ranked database for structural family-level enrichment analysis.")`

```{r export}
# LSEA Export Math ---------------------------
# Compile the full ranked lists into a single payload
lsea_payload <- list(
  testable_stats = ranked_results_list,
  ultra_sparse_background = ultra_sparse_lipids
)

# Save the unified payload
export_path <- file.path(output_dir_data, "LSEA_unified_payload.rds")
saveRDS(lsea_payload, export_path)

# Cleanup ---------------------------
#rm(lsea_payload, ranked_results_list)
```

# Appendix

`r what_this_does("Appended the raw underlying R code from the custom lipid omics engines to the HTML report's appendix to ensure complete analytical transparency and reproducibility.")`

```{r print_engine, code=readLines(here::here("code", "03_lipidomics_engines.R")), eval=FALSE}

```

```{r complete}
cat("Script 04b Complete! \n")
```
